name: From Arco da Rua Augusta to Parque Eduardo VII — route in Lisboa
name_original: Do Arco da Rua Augusta ao Parque Eduardo VII — percurso em Lisboa
region_intro: Hiking around Madalena offers exploration of a landscape shaped by volcanic activity, featuring dramatic scenery and unique natural formations. The region is dominated by Pico Mountain, Portugal's highest peak, and characterized by black volcanic rocks and fertile soils. Hikers can traverse diverse habitats, including the UNESCO World Heritage vineyards of Criação Velha, which are protected by intricate basalt walls. The coastline also presents natural swimming pools formed by lava deposits, adding to the varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Madalena The most popular hiking route is From Arco da Rua Augusta to Parque Eduardo VII — route in Lisboa, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 357 feet (109 meters). Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa Luzia viewpoint – Alfama District loop from Terreiro do Paço, a difficult 13.2 miles (21.3 km) path. This trail includes an elevation gain of 949 feet (289 meters) and offers a challenging experience. Local hikers also love the Santa Luzia viewpoint – View of Castelo de São Jorge loop from Lisbon, a 1.5 miles (2.5 km) trail leading through urban and scenic areas, often completed in about 42 minutes. Hiking around Madalena is defined by volcanic landscapes, coastal paths, and vineyard trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Madalena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. More than 14000 hikers have used komoot to explore Madalena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Madalena?
A: Madalena offers a diverse range of hiking experiences, primarily shaped by its unique volcanic landscape. You can explore challenging ascents up &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mountain&lt;/a&gt;, Portugal's highest peak, traverse the intricate UNESCO World Heritage vineyards of Criação Velha, or enjoy scenic coastal paths that lead to natural swimming pools formed by lava deposits. The region features trails suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Madalena area?
A: There are over 14 hiking routes documented around Madalena. These include 10 easy trails perfect for a leisurely stroll, 2 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 2 difficult paths, such as the demanding ascent of Pico Mountain, for experienced hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Madalena?
A: Madalena benefits from a mild climate year-round, making it an excellent destination for hiking in any season. However, for the most comfortable conditions and best visibility, especially for higher elevations like Pico Mountain, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) are often recommended.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Madalena?
A: Yes, Madalena offers several easy trails that are suitable for families. These often include walks through the vineyards or along the coastline, providing gentle terrain and beautiful scenery without significant elevation changes. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' for the most family-friendly experiences.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Madalena?
A: While many trails in natural areas across the Azores are generally dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for each trail, especially within protected areas or nature reserves. Keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for while hiking?
A: Hikers in Madalena will encounter stunning natural features. The dominant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mountain&lt;/a&gt; is a must-see, offering panoramic views from its summit. The UNESCO World Heritage vineyards of Criação Velha, with their unique basalt walls, are a cultural and natural highlight. Along the coast, you'll find natural lava pools and the picturesque Madalena Islets. The Gruta das Torres lava tube also offers a fascinating glimpse into the island's volcanic past.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Madalena?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Madalena are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. These are ideal for exploring specific areas like the vineyard landscapes or coastal sections without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on Madalena's hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Madalena is predominantly volcanic, characterized by black volcanic rocks and fertile soils. Trails can range from well-trodden paths through vineyards to rocky and uneven surfaces on mountain ascents. Coastal walks may feature paved sections or natural rock formations. Sturdy hiking boots are recommended for most trails.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Madalena?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For popular starting points like Casa da Montanha (for Pico Mountain), designated parking areas are usually available. For other trails, especially those in more rural or vineyard areas, you may find smaller parking spots or need to park responsibly along roadsides. It's advisable to check specific trail information for parking details.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Madalena?
A: The hiking trails around Madalena are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 1100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic volcanic scenery, the unique vineyard landscapes, and the rewarding views from higher elevations, particularly &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the trails offering scenic vistas?
A: Madalena is rich in stunning viewpoints. The summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mountain&lt;/a&gt; offers unparalleled panoramic views of the island and neighboring Azores islands on clear days. Coastal trails and elevated sections within the vineyards also provide picturesque vistas of the ocean, the Madalena Islets, and the unique cultural landscape.

Q: What is the elevation gain typically like on Madalena's hikes?
A: Elevation gain varies significantly depending on the trail. Easy coastal or vineyard walks may have minimal elevation changes. Moderate trails can involve several hundred feet of ascent. The most challenging hikes, such as the ascent of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mountain&lt;/a&gt;, involve substantial elevation gains, reaching over 1,100 meters (3,600 feet) from base to summit, requiring good physical condition.
tips: Pier from the end of the s. XVIII, with 2 columns that flank some marble stairs and views of the sunset next to the Tagus river.

Praça do Comércio (Commerce Square, better known as Terreiro do Paço), was the land where the Royal Palace of Lisbon sat for more than 200 years. It is one of the most important squares in Lisbon and, with a great view of the Tagus estuary, it is the nerve center of the city.

The Arch of Rua Augusta is the unmistakable entrance to the Plaza del Comercio from the street that bears the same name - the main artery of the Baixa - and had been closed for some time due to remodeling works in which the Lisbon Tourism Association was he has spent almost a million Euros.

The Arial is huge and totally impressive. It's hard to resist a certain magic. No wonder that festivals etc. take place here. are held frequently. The view of the city, which you ideally have “hiked” beforehand, is also magically beautiful.

An oasis of calm in the middle of the city.
Winter opening hours 9:00-17:00
Summer opening times 10:00-19:00

In the center of the square stands a monument to the Marquis of Pombal, one of the key figures in the history of Lisbon and all of Portugal. On the monument you can see a large figure of the marquis who was the governor of Lisbon between 1750 and 1777, along with a lion, the classic symbol of power.

The Ascensor da Gloria, popularly known as the Elevador da Gloria, is located in Lisbon, the capital of Portugal. It is one of the funiculars operated by Carris, and connects the Baixa to the Baixa Alto. Of these, it is the most used, carrying more than 3 million passengers annually.

Pedro IV Square, better known by its old name of Rossio, is a square located in Baixa, the nerve center of Lisbon for several centuries. Trials, shows, festivals, military parades and autos-da-fe took place there during the Inquisition. In Roman times there was a circus.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plaza_Pedro_IV

The Santa Justa Elevator, also called Elevador do Carmo, is an elevator that connects the Baixa Pombalina and Chiado neighborhoods in Lisbon, Portugal. It rises on Santa Justa Street and links this central promenade with Praza do Carmo, next to the Carmo Archaeological Museum.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elevador_de_Santa_Justa
